(a) The State Lottery Commission, and not the Ohio Casino Control Commission, has the authority to license and regulate video lottery sales agents, video lottery sales agent employees, and video lottery technology providers and to regulate the operation of video lottery terminals, in accordance with the provisions of Chapters 3770. and 3772. of the Revised Code, as amended by this act.

(b) The State Lottery Commission has the sole authority to issue, deny, suspend, or revoke video lottery sales agent, video lottery sales agent employee, and video lottery technology provider licenses.

(c) The Ohio Casino Control Commission may adopt, amend, or rescind rules under Chapter 119. of the Revised Code with respect to the regulation of video lottery sales agents, video lottery sales agent employees, video lottery technology providers, and the operation of video lottery terminals. The State Lottery Commission may adopt, amend, or rescind rules under Chapter 119. of the Revised Code that are not in conflict with the rules of the Ohio Casino Control Commission.

(d) All fees received under Chapter 3770. of the Revised Code, as amended by this act, shall be deposited in the state treasury to the credit of the State Lottery Gross Revenue Fund established under section 3770.06 of the Revised Code.

(2) As soon as practicable after the effective date of this section, the State Lottery Commission, in cooperation with the Ohio Casino Control Commission, shall issue guidance to video lottery sales agents, video lottery sales agent employees, and video lottery technology providers, explaining the transition of authority required by this act and the other requirements of this act that apply to those persons.

(3) On March 31, 2026, the authority to license and regulate video lottery sales agents, video lottery sales agent employees, and video lottery technology providers and to regulate the operation of video lottery terminals is transferred from the State Lottery Commission to the Ohio Casino Control Commission. The State Lottery Commission retains the authority to conduct video lottery terminal games in accordance with the provisions of Chapter 3770. of the Revised Code, as amended by this act. All records of the State Lottery Commission with respect to the licensing and regulation of video lottery sales agents, video lottery sales agent employees, and video lottery technology providers and the regulation of the operation of video lottery terminals shall be transferred to the Commission, except as otherwise necessary to facilitate the conduct of video lottery terminal games by the State Lottery Commission. The Ohio Casino Control Commission is successor to, and assumes the obligations of, the State Lottery Commission with respect to the licensing and regulation of video lottery sales agents, video lottery sales agent employees, and video lottery technology providers and the operation of video lottery terminals.

(B) Subject to Section 8 of this act, any valid video lottery sales agent, video lottery sales agent employee, or video lottery technology provider license issued by the State Lottery Commission before March 31, 2026, remains valid according to the terms of the license and is considered to be a license issued by the Ohio Casino Control Commission.

(C)(1) All rules of the State Lottery Commission with respect to the licensing and regulation of video lottery sales agents, video lottery sales agent employees, and video lottery technology providers and the operation of video lottery terminals, other than rules with respect to the conduct of video lottery terminal games, that are in effect immediately before March 31, 2026, continue in effect until rescinded or amended by the Ohio Casino Control Commission.

(2) Any rules that are pending before the Common Sense Initiative Office or the Joint Committee on Agency Rule Review on March 31, 2026, that were proposed by the State Lottery Commission under Chapter 3770. of the Revised Code, as that chapter existed immediately before the effective date of this section, with respect to the licensing and regulation of video lottery sales agents, video lottery sales agent employees, and video lottery technology providers and the operation of video lottery terminals, other than rules with respect to the conduct of video lottery terminal games, shall be treated as having been proposed by the Ohio Casino Control Commission.

(D) Any business commenced, but not completed, by the State Lottery Commission on March 31, 2026, with respect to the licensing and regulation of video lottery sales agents, video lottery sales agent employees, and video lottery technology providers and the operation of video lottery terminals, other than with respect to the conduct of video lottery terminal games, shall be completed by the Ohio Casino Control Commission in the same manner, and with the same effect, as if completed by the State Lottery Commission. No validation, cure, right, privilege, remedy, obligation, or liability is lost or impaired by reason of the transfer required by this section.

(E) On and after March 31, 2026, wherever the State Lottery Commission is referred to in any law, contract, or other document with respect to the licensing and regulation of video lottery sales agents, video lottery sales agent employees, and video lottery technology providers and the operation of video lottery terminals, other than with respect to the conduct of video lottery terminal games, the reference shall be deemed to refer to the Ohio Casino Control Commission.

(F) No action or proceeding pending on March 31, 2026, is affected by the transfer, and any such action or proceeding with respect to the licensing and regulation of video lottery sales agents, video lottery sales agent employees, and video lottery technology providers and the operation of video lottery terminals, other than with respect to the conduct of video lottery terminal games, shall be prosecuted or defended in the name of the Ohio Casino Control Commission. In all such actions and proceedings, the Ohio Casino Control Commission, on application to the court, shall be substituted as a party.

Section 7. (A) Notwithstanding any provision of law to the contrary, on or after the effective date of this section, the Director of Budget and Management shall make budget and accounting changes to implement the transfer of duties, functions, and programs to the Ohio Casino Control Commission as described in this act, including administrative organization, program transfers, renaming of funds, creation of new funds, transfer of state funds, and consolidation of funds.

(B) The Director may, if necessary, cancel or establish encumbrances or parts of encumbrances in fiscal years 2026 and 2027 in the appropriate funds and appropriation items for the same purposes and for payment to the same vendor. Such encumbrances are hereby appropriated. If necessary for the continued efficient administration of gambling regulation as provided in this act, the Director of Budget and Management may transfer appropriations between the Ohio Casino Control Commission, the Attorney General, the State Racing Commission, and the State Lottery Commission to continue levels of program services and efficiently deliver state funding to those programs.

Section 8. (A) Notwithstanding any contrary provision of this act, an individual who holds any of the following licenses as of the effective date of this section is considered to hold a key gaming employee license under section 3772.13 of the Revised Code or a gaming employee license under section 3772.131 of the Revised Code, as applicable, until the original license expires:

(1) A totalizator company management supervisory employee, totalizator company employee, or mutuel employee license issued by the State Racing Commission under Chapter 3769. of the Revised Code;

(2) A video lottery sales agent employee license issued by the State Lottery Commission under Chapter 3770:2 of the Ohio Administrative Code;

(3) A key employee license or casino employee license issued by the Ohio Casino Control Commission under Chapter 3772. of the Revised Code;

(4) A sports gaming occupational license issued by the Ohio Casino Control Commission under Chapter 3775. of the Revised Code.

(B) The term of any license described in division (A) of this section that expired not more than ninety days before the effective date of this section, or that would have expired within one hundred eighty days after the effective date of this section, is extended by one hundred eighty days.

(C) If, as of the effective date of this section, an individual holds two or more licenses described in division (A) of this section, the existing term of each license is extended through the latest expiration date of any such license the individual holds, as extended under division (B) of this section if applicable.

(D) After a license described in division (A) of this section expires, the licensee is required to hold a single key gaming employee license issued under section 3772.13 of the Revised Code or a single gaming employee license issued under section 3772.131 of the Revised Code, as applicable, in order to perform the licensee's duties.

(E) The Ohio Casino Control Commission, in cooperation with the State Racing Commission and the State Lottery Commission, shall issue guidance to licensees to facilitate the operation of this section.

Section 9. (A) The Executive Director of the Ohio Casino Control Commission shall designate a launch date for internet gambling that is as soon as practicable after the effective date of this section and not later than March 31, 2026. No person shall offer internet gambling in this state before the launch date.

(B)(1) The Executive Director of the Ohio Casino Control Commission shall set a series of deadlines by which a person must apply for an internet gambling license under Chapter 3771. of the Revised Code, as enacted by this act, and meet all other requirements for the license, in order to begin operating under the license on the launch date.

(2) If a person applies for an internet gambling license after the application deadline, the Commission is not required to review the application in time to issue the person a license before the launch date. If a person applies for an internet gambling license on or before the application deadline, but fails to meet any other requirement for the license by the applicable deadline to do so, the Commission is not required to issue the person a license before the launch date.

(C) During the period beginning on the effective date of this section and ending on October 1, 2027, all of the following apply:

(1) At the request of an applicant for an internet gambling license under Chapter 3771. of the Revised Code, as enacted by this act, the Executive Director of the Ohio Casino Control Commission may issue a provisional internet gambling license of the applicable type to the applicant, so long as the applicant has submitted a completed application for the license, including paying the required application fee. The Commission may prescribe by rule the requirements to receive a provisional internet gambling license, including additional application and license fees.

(2) In evaluating a request for a provisional internet gambling license, the Executive Director may consider the applicant's apparent eligibility for an internet gambling license under Chapter 3771. of the Revised Code, as enacted by this act, including whether the applicant has previously undergone a suitability investigation similar to the investigation the applicant must undergo to receive the internet gambling license.

(3) The Executive Director shall determine the period for which a provisional internet gambling license is valid, provided that the period shall not exceed three months. The Executive Director may renew a provisional internet gambling license for one additional period not to exceed three months.

Section 10. (A) The amendment and enactment by this act of sections 718.031, 3123.89, 3123.90, 3770.071, 3770.072, 3770.073, 3772.37, and 5747.063 of the Revised Code applies to amounts deducted and withheld from winnings from pari-mutuel wagering on horse racing, video lottery terminal gaming, casino gaming, and sports gaming on and after March 31, 2026.

(B) The amendment by this act of section 5753.021 of the Revised Code applies to sports gaming receipts received on and after the first day of January after the effective date of this section.

Section 11. Notwithstanding any contrary provision of division (F) of section 3772.02 of the Revised Code, as amended by this act, the change in compensation required by this act under that division first applies to a member of the Ohio Casino Control Commission when the member begins a new term as a member of the Commission on or after the effective date of this section.

Section 12. The General Assembly, applying the principle stated in division (B) of section 1.52 of the Revised Code that amendments are to be harmonized if reasonably capable of simultaneous operation, finds that the following sections, presented in this act as composites of the sections as amended by the acts indicated, are the resulting versions of the sections in effect prior to the effective date of the sections as presented in this act:

Section 119.12 of the Revised Code as amended by both H.B. 33 and S.B. 21 of the 135th General Assembly.

Section 3772.03 of the Revised Code as amended by H.B. 29 of the 134th General Assembly and both H.B. 49 and H.B. 132 of the 132nd General Assembly.

Section 3772.99 of the Revised Code as amended by both H.B. 32 and H.B. 49 of the 132nd General Assembly.

Section 5747.01 of the Revised Code as amended by both H.B. 101 and S.B. 154 of the 135th General Assembly.
